meaning: n.<俗>怪(老)人,有怪癖的(老)人
examples: Millin was a crusty old codger who took the college job after he retired from the navy.
There are suits on phones, a confused crowd of witnesses and a codger rifling through filing cabinets.
The old codger may need a cane to walk, but not for his mind, which is still sharp as a razor blade .
Levy, age 52 and a codger by Web standards, grew up collecting baseball autographs at spring training camps in Florida.
She proudly talks about how hard it was to be a geek when she was growing up with all the tact of a codger trying to get kids off her lawn.
